revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Bentenyama Kofun]][[ja:弁天山古墳 (富津市)]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Fottsu, Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Bentenyama kofun | native_name =弁天山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = Bentenyama kofun | map_type = Japan Chiba Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Bentenyama kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.281833|N|139.858306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Futtsu, Chiba|Futtsu, Chiba]], {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Bentenyama kofun'''|弁天山古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in what is now the Kokubo neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Futtsu, Chiba|Futtsu]], {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1929.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138177|title=弁天山古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Bentenyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the southwestern part of the {{ill|Bōsō Peninsula|en|Bōsō Peninsula|qid=Q1022115|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|1=Bōsō Peninsula}}, on a low plateau overlooking {{ill|Tokyo Bay|en|Tokyo Bay|qid=Q141017|lt=Tokyo Bay|1=Tokyo Bay}} near the mouth of the Iwase and Kokubo river just south of {{ill|Cape Futtsu|en|Cape Futtsu|qid=Q11457112|lt=Cape Futtsu|1=Cape Futtsu}}. It has an overall length of approximately 87.5 meters and was once surrounded by a wide moat. The tumulus suffered considerable damage at the end of {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} when {{ill|Sanuki Domain|en|Sanuki Domain|qid=Q3034518|lt=Sanuki Domain|1=Sanuki Domain}} was transferred to {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|qid=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}, and cut away a portion of the tumulus to make space for the construction of a {{ill|Han school|en|Han school|qid=Q3578015|lt=Han school|1=Han school}}. A {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} was constructed on top of the circular portion of the tumulus. In 1927, during the construction of ten neighboring elementary school, this shrine and a portion of the circular portion of the tumulus collapsed, and the pit-type [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was revealed. It was found to have megalithic stone walls and ceiling, with a length of 4.9 meters. A stone placed in the center of the ceiling has a lanyard protrusion, and it attracted attention as this feature is rarely seen in ''kofun'' outside of {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}. This was used to lower the lid stone of the sarcophagus. [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s included iron swords and spears, fragments of armor and a helm, and a large number of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. From these relics, the construction period of the tumulus is considered to be in the latter half of the 5th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> A survey for conservation and maintenance to prevent further collapse of the tumulus was conducted in 1971 and in 1975 to 1976. Concrete stairs provide public access to the top of the tumulus, and the stone sarcophagus and its lid are on display at a small adjacent building. The tumulus is about 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Onuki Station|Onuki Station]] on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Uchibō Line|en|Uchibō Line|qid=Q1142964|lt=Uchibō Line|1=Uchibō Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ;Total length: 86 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 50 meters wide x 5.5 meters high, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 53 meter diameter x 5.5 meters high, 2-tier ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)|qid=Q6573257|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Chiba)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== *[https://www.pref.chiba.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/bunkazai/p411-001-1.html Chiba Prefecture official site] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.city.futtsu.lg.jp/0000000782.html Futtsu City official home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
[[en:Bentenyama Kofun]]
